Viability status: Fresh / likely good; germination test advised
Storage method: Sealed plastic packet with tissue and silica gel; keep dry and cool.
Storage temperature: Cool dry storage with silica gel
Pretreatment needed: Cold moist stratification recommended; soak 12-24h then stratify 3-6 weeks before sowing.
Germination instructions: After stratification, sow shallowly in free-draining sterile seed mix; keep lightly moist, not waterlogged; test 10-15 seeds first.
Adult plant image source: Use app fallback or curated Wikimedia/Wikidata image of adult Atlas cedar
Species notes: Evergreen conifer from the Atlas Mountains; outdoor hardy when established, seedlings need protection.
Label transcription: Cedrus atlantica - Atlas cedar / Kew Gardens - London / 12/06/2026 / ~100 seeds
Research / source notes: Label corrected to Cedrus atlantica with lowercase species epithet; Atlas cedar is a Pinaceae conifer native to the Atlas Mountains.
